title: Trump 48-Hour Hormuz Ultimatum
market_impact: Escalation risk bullish for oil (CL) and gold (GC). SPY bearish on military action risk. Energy sector (XLE, USO) sensitive.
notes: Posted Mar 21 7:44 PM ET. Deadline Mar 23 7:44 PM ET. Threatened to obliterate Iranian power plants starting with largest. Postponed multiple times → superseded by Apr 6 deadline.

title: Trump Energy Strike Pause Deadline
market_impact: If deadline passes without deal: US strikes on Iran power grid resume. Bullish oil (CL) and gold (GC). Bearish SPY. Energy sector (XLE, USO, UNG) highly sensitive. Desalination plants at risk affects Gulf states.
notes: Truth Social Mar 26: 'I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 PM Eastern Time.' 3rd postponement of original Mar 23 Hormuz ultimatum. Talks 'going very well' per Trump; Iran denies negotiations. 15-point US ceasefire proposal rejected by Tehran. IRGC naval chief Tangsiri killed by Israel Mar 26. G7 foreign ministers meeting in Paris to discuss Hormuz.

title: Trump Iran War Address — 'Hit Them Extremely Hard'
market_impact: Crude oil +13% ($113/bbl WTI, $109 Brent). S&P 500 -1.3%, Dow -600pts, Nasdaq -1.7%. No de-escalation timeline. 2-3 more weeks of heavy strikes pledged. Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted. Gas prices heading toward $4.50-$5/gal by Memorial Day.
notes: 19-min prime-time address Apr 1. First formal speech since war began 5 weeks ago. Claimed 'core strategic objectives nearing completion' but committed to 2-3 more weeks of strikes. Iran responded with missile attacks on Israel and Gulf states. IG analyst: 'Instead of no more war, we got no, more war.' Markets reversed Mon-Tue rally. Oil back above $100 after briefly dipping below.

title: Liberation Day 1-Year Anniversary — Tariff Fallout
market_impact: SCOTUS invalidated some tariffs (Feb 2026) — refund process underway for billions in collected duties. Ongoing supply chain cost inflation. Consumer prices still elevated from tariff pass-through.
notes: One year since Trump's Apr 2 2025 Liberation Day tariffs. Supreme Court struck down tariffs imposed under emergency powers (Feb 2026). Refund process ongoing. CFR, LA Times, Marketplace all running retrospectives on economic damage.
